chore: remove optional files and development tooling

Remove non-essential files to streamline the repository:
- .editorconfig - Editor preferences (optional)
- .prettierignore - Prettier formatter exclusions (optional)
- xo.config.ts - Linter configuration (optional)
- .husky/ - Git pre-commit hooks (optional, can interfere with workflow)
- tests/ - Unit tests (optional for standalone bot)
- .github/ - GitHub issue templates and workflows (optional)

These files are not required for the bot to function. Users can configure
their own linting, formatting, and testing if needed.

Minimal dependencies remain: source code, package.json, tsconfig.json,
esbuild.config.mjs, and .env configuration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(expenses): fix NOT NULL constraint crash when saving manual/reduction expenses

Root cause: explicit null values for NOT NULL DEFAULT columns bypass PostgreSQL DEFAULT:
  - extraction_warnings: NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::text[]
  - requires_user_confirmation: NOT NULL DEFAULT false

These were set to: input.x ?? null — which inserts explicit NULL even when the
caller doesn't provide the field. PostgreSQL only uses DEFAULT when the column is
OMITTED from the INSERT, not when NULL is explicitly given.

Fix: use spread pattern to omit when value is absent, letting DB DEFAULT apply.
Same pattern already applied to cost_type in migration 122 fix.

Also:
- Add reductionError state + error display in reduction form (was silently failing)
- Clear reduction error on cancel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: cross-namespace KopiaRepository lookup in controllers

Both the kopiabackup and pvc-watcher controllers were only looking
for KopiaRepository in the backup/PVC's namespace. When the repo
lives in a different namespace (e.g. kopia-operator) while backups
are in app namespaces (e.g. hass), the repo was never found.

Now both controllers first check the local namespace, then fall back
to listing all KopiaRepositories cluster-wide. An error is returned
if no match or multiple matches are found (ambiguous).

* fix(layout): preserve axis alignment in Midgaard zone layout

BFS correctly places east/west-connected rooms on the same row, but
force-directed relaxation and subsequent refinement phases broke this
alignment. The wall-road branch pulled inside-the-west-gate 4 cells
off the main-street row.

Two complementary fixes:

1. relaxationScore() — proportional diagonal penalty (DIAGONAL_PENALTY *
   max(offAxis, 1)) used only during relaxation phases. Makes large
   off-axis displacements progressively more expensive so the optimizer
   won't drag aligned rooms toward distant neighbours.

2. moveWouldBreakAlignment() — guard function that rejects moves breaking
   axis alignment between adjacent cardinal neighbours. Applied to
   relaxation, diagonal cascade, and direction-violation repair phases.
   For E/W exits protects Y alignment; for N/S exits protects X alignment.

* feat(layout): post-BFS cardinal alignment pass

Add Phase 5c to computeLayout — a cardinal alignment pass that runs
after direction violation repair and before occlusion fix.

Problem: BFS visit order is entry-point-sensitive. When the zone
designer uses a different entry room than the zone's actual entry
(e.g. due to room type mismatch between 'entrance' and 'entry'),
east/west-connected rooms can end up at different y levels, creating
visual right-angles where straight corridors should appear.

Solution: Build union-find groups for E/W exits (rooms that must
share the same y) and N/S exits (rooms that must share the same x).
For each group with misaligned coordinates, batch-shift all outlier
rooms plus their perpendicular subtrees to the majority coordinate.
Accept the shift only if the global layout score improves.

The cascade-shift approach preserves north/south topology: when an
E/W-misaligned room moves to match its row, its entire N/S subtree
moves with it, keeping wall-road correctly south of the gate.

Test: new entry-point independence test verifies E/W alignment holds
across 5 different BFS entry points on the same graph.

* fix(layout): general cardinal alignment without zone-specific logic

Replace the fragile cascade guard in Phase 5c cardinal alignment with
two general-purpose improvements:

1. Group-aware cascade: when the perpendicular subtree BFS encounters
   a room belonging to another multi-room alignment group, pull in the
   ENTIRE group so their internal alignment is preserved during shifts.

2. Chain-push collision resolution: instead of findNearestUnoccupied
   (which can displace rooms sideways, breaking alignments), push
   colliding rooms 1 step further in the shift direction (domino
   style), preserving relative ordering along the perpendicular axis.

3. Alignment-specific acceptance: use misaligned-pair count instead of
   general layoutScore, so alignment improvements aren't rejected by
   unrelated distance increases.

4. Largest-first processing: groups sorted by size so the most
   constrained corridors (e.g. 9-room main street) get priority.

All changes are topology-driven — no zone names, no hardcoded slugs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(layout): detect and resolve criss-crossing edges

Add Phase 5d to the BFS layout engine that detects and resolves
edge crossings — where a horizontal edge segment intersects a
vertical edge segment at a point that is not a room position.

Detection: builds orthogonal edge segments per z-level, tests all
pairs for perpendicular intersection at non-room points.

Resolution: for each crossing, tries repositioning the least-
connected endpoint room to a nearby cell that eliminates the
crossing without breaking cardinal alignment, direction semantics,
or creating diagonal edges. Uses score-based acceptance (total
crossing count must decrease).

Runs after cardinal alignment (Phase 5c) and before occlusion fix
(Phase 6). All 28 layout tests + 13 ELK tests pass, including the
Midgaard regression test.

* feat(map): add Phase 5e selective row/column insertion for edge crossings

Add Phase 5e to computeLayout.ts as a fallback strategy after Phase 5d
(individual room moves). When edge crossings remain, Phase 5e tries
selective row/column insertion: for each H-V crossing at point (vX, hY),
compute 4 partition strategies (rooms above/below hY shift up/down,
rooms left/right of vX shift left/right). Try smallest partition first.
Apply shift temporarily, verify no diagonals/collisions, count crossings,
rollback if no improvement.

Key insight: when moving a GROUP of rooms, alignment checking must use
NEW neighbor positions (not old), so we apply moves first then check for
diagonals using updated positions.

  cardinal alignment + zero diagonals — further reduction not achievable)
- Test 21 (Siltgate): still 0 diagonals (critical regression test)

* fix(layout): reduce crossings via row/column exchange in Phase 5e/9

Rewrite fixCrossingsByInsertion() to use full row/column exchange
instead of alignment-group shifts with chain-push cascades.

For each H×V crossing, swap ALL rooms on the H-edge's row with ALL
rooms on the V-edge's nearest endpoint row. This is collision-free
by construction and preserves E-W/N-S alignment within each row.

Key changes:
- Row/column exchange approach: swap entire coordinate lines, sorted
  by distance (prefer adjacent)
- Neighborhood-aware mismatch guard: counts mismatches for moved
  rooms AND their direct neighbors, catching bystander violations
- Two-mode acceptance: strict (Phase 5e, no mismatch increase) and
  weighted (Phase 9, accept if crossingsFixed >= mismatchDelta)
- Remove all diagnostic console.log statements

Results: Midgaard crossings reduced from 9 to 5.
All 29 tests pass including Siltgate zero-diagonal constraint.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace row exchange with true grid expansion for crossing fixes

Phase 5e/9 now resolves edge crossings by inserting blank rows/columns
rather than swapping existing rows. For each H×V crossing:

1. Shift a partition of the grid to create a gap OUTSIDE the crossing
   edge's span (e.g. shift rooms above V's top by -1)
2. Move the crossing edge's alignment group into the gap

This genuinely adds space to the grid at crossing points. The partition
shift preserves all internal alignment; only the moved group's jump
can create direction mismatches (guarded by weighted acceptance).

Results: Midgaard crossings 9→3 (improved from 5 with exchange approach).
All 29 tests pass including Siltgate zero-diagonal constraint.

commit.message:
fix: per-channel FX now works — two root causes found and fixed

Root cause 1: canUseParameterUpdatePath compared ALL enabled effects
against the current chain. When selectedChannels changed (same effect
count/ID/type), it took the fast path and never reached the globalEffects
filter — keeping the effect in the serial master chain.

Fix: Compare only GLOBAL effects (no selectedChannels) against current
chain. When an effect moves from global→channel-targeted, the count
mismatches and forces a full rebuild.

Root cause 2: ToneEngine.rebuildMasterEffects() tore down ALL channel
isolation on every master chain rebuild. So the useEffect that fires
after selectedChannels changes would destroy the isolation that
scheduleWasmEffectRebuild just created.

Fix: Remove the legacy teardown code. Channel isolation is now managed
exclusively by ChannelRoutedEffects via scheduleWasmEffectRebuild.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: session fetch stuck — remove isLoadingSession from useEffect deps

The useEffect that fetches the ACS token had isLoadingSession in its
dependency array. When setIsLoadingSession(true) fired inside the async
callback, React re-ran the effect cleanup setting isActive=false, which
silently discarded the API response. Users saw 'Minting a secure join
token...' forever.

Fix: use a ref (fetchingRef) to guard against duplicate fetches instead
of including the loading flag in deps. Also added:
- Adapter-loading spinner ('Connecting to the meeting room...')
- Retry button on error states
- Animated spinners for both loading phases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Docs(plan): fix retry backoff timing in contract

Remove phantom 8s delay: only 3 sleeps between 4
attempts (1s+2s+4s=7s). Update max wait from 15s to 7s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

trading: extract per-position close with abort policy

Extract _execute_single_close() from the bulk close loop in
_close_positions(), separating fill-mode retry, tick refresh on
requote, PnL metadata assembly, and comment fallback into a
self-contained helper.

Add consecutive-failure abort policy: when 3 positions fail in a row
the remaining positions are skipped with a clear abort marker,
preventing unbounded retry against a dead broker connection.

New tests:
- _execute_single_close success, no-tick, unknown-side
- abort after consecutive failures
- abort counter resets on success
